papers.title_en: Task-Adaptive Design of Modular Aerial Manipulators Under Airflow Exposure Constraints
papers.evidence: 空中机械臂设计与气流约束
papers.tldr: 多旋翼无人机空中操作时，旋翼气流对敏感目标构成限制。本文提出一个基于优化的模块化空中机械臂设计框架，联合考虑任务扳手可行性、末端执行器位置和气流暴露约束。通过锥-球包络近似旋翼气流，框架可适应不同任务要求，同时满足目标侧气流暴露和平台内气流干扰约束。不同于固定末端位置的现有设计，该框架优化了末端位置与平台配置，实验验证了其有效性。
papers.abstract_en: Aerial manipulation with multirotor platforms enables physical interaction in complex environments, but rotor-induced airflow remains a critical limitation for tasks involving airflow-sensitive targets or surroundings. This paper presents an optimization-based design framework for modular aerial manipulators that jointly considers task wrench feasibility, end-effector placement, and airflow exposure constraints. We first introduce a novel categorization of target-side airflow tolerance and formulate the corresponding exposure requirements as geometric constraints. To efficiently model rotor-induced airflow, we introduce a compact cone-sphere envelope that approximates the spreading structure of a quadrotor's airflow while preserving computational tractability for optimization. Building on this formulation, we propose a reconfiguration optimization that adapts a modular aerial manipulator to diverse task wrench requirements while enforcing both target-side airflow exposure and intra-platform airflow interference constraints. Unlike prior designs that assume a fixed end-effector location, the proposed framework optimizes the end-effector placement together with the platform configuration. Scalability experiments and ablation studies validate the effectiveness of the proposed framework.

papers.title_en: Intercepting an Agile Target with Net-Carrying Drones using Competitive Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
papers.evidence: 多智能体强化学习用于携带网兜的无人机拦截敏捷目标
papers.tldr: 针对敏捷无人机拦截难题，本文提出用携带网的无人机团队执行拦截任务，并建模为竞争多智能体强化学习。采用MAPPO结合PFSP训练追捕者和逃逸者，使用低层集体推力和机体角速率控制实现敏捷飞行。仿真中该方法相比启发式基线在捕获率、时间和碰撞率上均更优。消融实验表明PFSP增强了策略的鲁棒性，低层控制对学习执行性策略至关重要，定性分析还揭示了追捕者间的协同战术。
papers.abstract_en: This article presents a solution to intercept an agile drone by a team of agile drone carrying catching nets. We formulate the problem as a competitive Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) task. To address the problem of nonstationarity and catastrophic forgetting of agents overfitting to the current opponent strategy, we train the pursuers and the evader using Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization (MAPPO) with Prioritized Fictitious Self Play (PFSP). We train the agents in a high-fidelity simulator using low-level control commands, collective thrust and body rates (CTBR), to achieve agile flights for both the pursuers and the evader. We compare the performance of the trained policies in terms of catch rate, time to catch and crash rates, against heuristic baselines and show that our solution outperforms them. Ablation studies show that PFSP lead to more robust policies that can adapt to different opponent strategies, and that a low-level control commands are crucial for learning performing strategies in the pursuit-evasion task. Finally, a qualitative analysis of the learned behaviours highlights the emergence of cooperative tactics among the pursuers.
